original_hypothesis.title: PermissionsRegistry.removeRole may corrupt address role lists, breaking downstream authorization tooling and role revocation workflows
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When removing a role, the cleanup loop that edits _addressToRoles appears to use the global _roles array for swap-and-pop rather than the per-address role array, potentially writing an unrelated role into a user's role list. While hasRole is cleared, the view-based role enumeration becomes inconsistent and can cause operational errors (e.g., failing to revoke expected roles or misreporting permissions).
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Role registries are trust roots. Even if on-chain modifiers use hasRole mapping, corrupted enumeration impacts incident response, monitoring, and any contracts/off-chain systems that depend on addressToRole() for audits or revocation scripts.

proof_of_signal.pattern: removeRole(): `_addressToRoles[rta[i]][k] = _roles[_roles.length -1]; _addressToRoles[rta[i]].pop();` uses global _roles array element, not per-address last element

preconditions: At least one address had that role and also has other roles
Operational tooling relies on addressToRole() or _addressToRoles for remediation

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: incorrect array swap source during removal
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: address role lists become inconsistent with hasRole mapping and reality
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no invariant checks ensuring (role in address list) iff hasRole[role][addr]

analysis_questions: Can corrupted _addressToRoles later prevent removeRoleFrom from functioning correctly?
Do any in-scope contracts rely on addressToRole output (vs hasRole) for critical authorization?
Does the loop risk out-of-bounds by using `_roles[_roles.length-1]` after popping roles?
validation_requirements: Unit test: assign multiple roles to address, remove one role; assert addressToRole reflects correct remaining roles
Fuzz role removals and validate consistency with hasRole mapping

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: After a role is removed, affected addresses appear (via enumeration) to hold unrelated roles, causing admins/off-chain automation to mis-handle privilege revocation and incident response, potentially leaving real privileges in place longer than expected.
prerequisites: Off-chain tooling uses enumeration as source of truth

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Indirect: delayed revocation/misconfiguration can enable subsequent misuse of privileged actions
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Manual remediation by reassigning roles and redeploying tooling; contract data remains corrupted

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Likely missing role enumeration consistency tests

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Compare hasRole mapping vs addressToRole outputs after removeRole

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
